title: Rovaniemi Arctic Winter Escape.

trains.name: Trains & Taxi Transfers
trains.blurb: Private taxi transfers linking Rovaniemi airport, hotel and included Arctic excursions.

transport.1: Private transfer from Rovaniemi airport to the hotel on arrival.
transport.2: Private transfer to and from a working reindeer farm for a 1-hour safari, with an evening pickup for the guaranteed Northern Lights excursion.
transport.3: Private transfer to and from a husky farm for the husky sledding experience.
transport.6: Private transfer from the Rovaniemi hotel to the airport for departure.

inclusions: Private airport–hotel transfer on arrival in Rovaniemi
Reindeer Farm visit with 1-hour reindeer safari
Husky Experience with farm visit and short sledding safari
4 nights at Scandic Pohjanovi (or similar) on a Full Board basis

days.title: Arrive Rovaniemi
days.desc: Fly into Rovaniemi, gateway to Finnish Lapland and the official hometown of Santa Claus, sitting right on the Arctic Circle where the Kemijoki and Ounasjoki rivers meet. A private transfer takes you to your hotel, leaving the rest of the day free to adjust to the polar winter light and settle in.
days.taste: A glass of Lapin Kulta, Finland's own Arctic-brewed lager, is the classic first drink after checking in.

days.title: Reindeer Farm & Northern Lights
days.desc: Visit a working reindeer farm for a hands-on introduction to Lapland's traditional reindeer husbandry, followed by a short reindeer-pulled safari through snow-covered forest. After dark, head out on a guaranteed Northern Lights excursion away from the town's light pollution to watch for the Aurora Borealis dancing overhead.
days.taste: Poronkäristys — sautéed reindeer served with mashed potato and lingonberry sauce — is Lapland's signature dish and often the farm's own lunch offering.
days.experience: A guaranteed Aurora Borealis hunt is one of the defining experiences of a Lapland winter, best seen on clear nights between September and March.

days.title: Husky Experience
days.desc: Spend the day at a husky farm, meeting the dogs before a short sledding safari across frozen trails behind your own team of huskies. It is one of the quintessential Lapland winter activities and a highlight for most first-time visitors to the Arctic Circle.
days.taste: Warm up afterwards with glögi, the spiced and berry-sweetened mulled wine served at farms and cafés across Finland all winter.
days.experience: Mushing your own husky sled team through snowy taiga forest is a genuine Lapland classic, dating back to the region's traditional working dogs.

days.title: Rovaniemi at leisure
days.desc: A free day to explore Rovaniemi at your own pace. Visit the Arktikum museum, its glass-and-steel tunnel built into the riverbank to tell the story of Arctic life and science, or cross the Arctic Circle line itself at Santa Claus Village, just north of the town centre.
days.taste: Try leipäjuusto, Finland's traditional squeaky cheese, warmed and served with cloudberry jam in the town's cafés.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — optional Santa Claus Village visit or snowmobile safari available.

days.title: Rovaniemi at leisure
days.desc: Another free day in Rovaniemi to relax or add further Arctic activities such as ice fishing, a snowmobile safari, or a visit to a real reindeer or snow hotel in the surrounding forests.
days.experience: A traditional Finnish sauna followed by a plunge through the ice — avanto swimming — is a genuine local ritual, not just a tourist add-on.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — optional ice fishing or snowmobile safari available.

days.title: Depart Rovaniemi
days.desc: A private transfer takes you from your hotel to Rovaniemi airport in time for your onward or homebound flight, bringing your Arctic Circle winter break to a close.

about.title: Best season
about.body: This is a winter-only programme, best enjoyed between November and March when snow, huskies and Northern Lights excursions are all in season.

about.title: Weather
about.body: Expect true Arctic winter conditions, typically -15°C to -5°C with heavy snow cover and short daylight hours.

about.title: Currency & docs
about.body: Finland uses the Euro (€) and is part of the Schengen Area, so no internal border checks apply for Schengen-area travellers.

goodToKnow.title: What to pack
goodToKnow.body: Thermal layers, insulated waterproof boots and warm gloves are essential — outdoor excursions run regardless of temperature.

goodToKnow.title: Daylight hours
goodToKnow.body: In deep winter Rovaniemi enters kaamos, the polar night, with only a few hours of daylight and a long blue twilight either side — plan sightseeing around the brightest midday hours.

goodToKnow.title: Northern Lights
goodToKnow.body: Sightings depend on clear skies and solar activity; the guaranteed excursion includes a repeat outing on request if the Aurora is not seen.

goodToKnow.title: Your transfers
goodToKnow.body: Airport, farm and departure transfers are private and run to your flight and excursion times.

goodToKnow.title: Money
goodToKnow.body: Cards are accepted almost everywhere in Finland, including for small purchases; carrying cash is rarely necessary, and tipping is appreciated but not customary.

terms: All rates are net and per person for the package.
Child rates apply for children aged 2 to 11 years old. One child sharing a room with 2 adults, subject to hotel policy.
All rates are subject to availability at the time of booking. Europe Incoming will endeavour to match the rates quoted.
Rates are not applicable during trade fair periods, major European public holidays, and other major events.
City taxes are not included in the price.
If certain attractions are closed during specific periods, alternative options will be arranged.
All bookings must be confirmed at least 60 working days prior to arrival.
100% pre-payment required by bank transfer or credit card (Visa or Mastercard). All bank transfer charges covered by the Agent.
Vouchers will be issued after receipt of full payment.
